items.title: Big-O Complexity
items.description: Binary search is the canonical O(log n) algorithm — the proof that halving the input changes the growth class.

items.title: Sorting Algorithms
items.description: Binary search requires sorted input; spending O(n log n) once to sort enables O(log n) lookups forever.

items.title: Hash Tables
items.description: Hash tables give O(1) average lookup but no order; binary search gives O(log n) and supports range queries. Pick by access pattern.

items.title: Trees & BSTs
items.description: A balanced BST is essentially binary search organized as a tree — every search is the same halving idea.

items.title: Recursion
items.description: Binary search has a natural recursive form; iterative is preferred to avoid Python's recursion limit on deep arrays.
